title: Quiz: Kraus Operators
description: Test your understanding of the operator-sum representation

questions.question: The Kraus representation of a CPTP map is:

options.text: E(rho) = sum_k K_k rho K_k^dag with sum K_k^dag K_k = I

questions.explanation: The Kraus representation is E(rho) = sum_k K_k rho K_k^dag where the completeness relation sum K_k^dag K_k = I ensures trace preservation.

questions.question: The amplitude damping channel models:

options.text: Energy decay from excited to ground state

questions.explanation: Amplitude damping models spontaneous emission or T1 decay, where the qubit decays from |1> to |0> with some probability.

questions.question: The Kraus decomposition of a quantum channel is unique.
questions.explanation: False! Kraus decomposition is not unique. Different sets of Kraus operators related by unitary transformations represent the same channel.

questions.question: For a unitary channel U(rho) = U rho U^dag, how many Kraus operators are needed?
questions.explanation: Unitary channels have rank-1 Choi matrix and require only 1 Kraus operator: K = U.

questions.question: The completeness relation sum_k K_k^dag K_k = I ensures:
questions.explanation: The completeness relation ensures Tr[E(rho)] = Tr[sum_k K_k rho K_k^dag] = Tr[rho sum_k K_k^dag K_k] = Tr[rho] = 1.

questions.question: The minimum number of Kraus operators equals:

options.text: The rank of the Choi matrix

questions.explanation: The minimum number of Kraus operators needed equals the rank of the channel's Choi matrix.

questions.question: For the depolarizing channel D_p with Kraus operators sqrt(1-p)I, sqrt(p/3)X, sqrt(p/3)Y, sqrt(p/3)Z, the channel is:
questions.explanation: The depolarizing channel represents a probabilistic mixture of identity (with probability 1-p) and the three Pauli operations (each with probability p/3).

questions.question: If {K_k} and {L_j} are two Kraus representations of the same channel, then L_j = sum_k U_jk K_k for some unitary U.
questions.explanation: True. Different Kraus representations of the same channel are related by unitary (or isometric) transformations acting on the Kraus index.
